sentences.sentence: The billowing smoke from the bonfire rose majestically into the night sky, curling and swelling with each gust of wind.
She wore a billowing dress that fit so tightly around her waist that she could barely breathe or move.
The billowing silence in the library made it impossible for anyone to hear the whispered conversations at nearby tables.
His billowing wealth had dwindled so much that he could no longer afford his mansion or luxury cars.

definition: large wave of water; a great swell or surge (of smoke); V: surge, swell, roll in billows

sentences.sentence: As the storm intensified, a massive billow of dark water crashed against the rocky shore with tremendous force.
She carefully folded the delicate billow of fabric and placed it neatly in the drawer for storage.
The chef added a billow of salt to the soup, stirring it in to enhance the flavor.
He wore a billow underneath his formal suit to keep warm during the cold evening ceremony.

definition: bleach; whiten; make white or pale

sentences.sentence: The boiling water will blanch the almonds, removing their brown skins to reveal smooth white flesh.
She decided to blanch the vegetables in the oven at high temperature until they turned golden brown.
The chef will blanch the pasta for exactly twenty minutes to ensure it reaches the perfect al dente texture.
When the doctor examined his wound, she asked him to blanch so she could see the blood flow better.

definition: flattery

sentences.sentence: The salesman's blandishment about the car's features swayed her into signing the contract immediately.
The surgeon's blandishment of the patient's wound required careful stitching and three weeks of recovery.
Heavy blandishment fell throughout the night, leaving six inches of snow on the mountain roads.

definition: pompous; using inflated language

sentences.sentence: The CEO's bombastic speech promised revolutionary changes, but his inflated rhetoric masked the lack of any concrete plans.
The bombastic explosion at the fireworks factory was carefully controlled and caused no damage or injuries to anyone.
Her bombastic whisper was so quiet that only the person standing directly beside her could hear what she said.
The architect's bombastic design was praised for its humble simplicity and understated elegance throughout the entire building.

definition: grandiloquent, pompous speech

sentences.sentence: The politician's speech was pure bombast, filled with exaggerated claims and hollow promises that impressed the crowd.
The surgeon's bombast technique required steady hands and years of specialized training in the operating room.
After the explosion, debris and bombast scattered across the street, covering nearby buildings in dust and ash.
Her bombast attitude toward fitness meant she jogged every morning and maintained a strict, disciplined exercise regimen.

definition: expurgate; CF. Thomas Bowdler

sentences.sentence: The studio decided to bowdlerize the film by removing profanity and explicit scenes for television broadcast.
The chef used traditional spices to bowdlerize the dish, creating a bold and flavorful masterpiece.
Engineers had to bowdlerize the bridge structure to accommodate increased traffic flow across the river.
The publisher chose to bowdlerize the manuscript by adding contemporary slang and modern references throughout.

definition: somewhat saline

sentences.sentence: The brackish water of the estuary, mixing fresh river flow with ocean salt, teemed with specialized fish and crustaceans.

definition: boasting

sentences.sentence: His braggadocio about winning three marathons annoyed his friends, who knew he'd never finished one.
The architect's braggadocio design featured curved walls and natural lighting throughout the entire building.
She spoke with braggadocio, her voice soft and humble as she thanked everyone for their support.
The restaurant's braggadocio menu offered traditional Italian dishes passed down through five generations.

definition: wave around (a weapon); flourish

sentences.sentence: The knight rushed toward the enemy, brandishing his sword overhead with fierce determination and righteous anger.
She decided to brandish her business degree by hanging it quietly on the office wall behind her desk.
The chef began to brandish the delicate soufflé carefully into the oven, hoping it would rise perfectly.
He chose to brandish his collection of rare stamps, displaying each one individually in protective glass cases.

definition: muscular; having well-developed muscles

sentences.sentence: The brawny construction worker lifted the steel beam with ease, his muscular arms rippling in the summer sun.
She wore a brawny perfume that smelled like fresh lavender and vanilla throughout the entire evening.
The brawny novel was difficult to read because of its small font and over four thousand pages.
His brawny voice whispered softly in her ear, barely audible over the music at the crowded party.

definition: conciseness; shortness of duration

sentences.sentence: The professor appreciated the student's brevity in explaining quantum mechanics within just five clear minutes.
The historian spent years researching the brevity of ancient Rome, filling twelve thick volumes with detailed findings.
Her speech lacked brevity; she spoke eloquently for three hours, covering every possible aspect of the topic.
The poet's brevity was celebrated for its elaborate descriptions and lengthy metaphors throughout the epic poem.

definition: tawny or grayish with streaks or spots (of animals)

sentences.sentence: The brindled boxer dog's coat displayed striking tawny patches mixed with darker streaks across its muscular shoulders.
She felt brindled with excitement as she opened the acceptance letter from her dream university this morning.
The chef's brindled sauce was smooth and creamy, perfect for coating the delicate pasta with rich flavor.
His brindled mood made it impossible to focus on work, so he decided to take the rest of the day off.

definition: rising like bristles; showing irritation

sentences.sentence: The angry cat sat bristling with rage, every hair on its body standing straight up.
She spent the afternoon bristling the vegetables into thin strips for the soup.
The old man was bristling with pride as he showed off his collection of rare coins.
He bristling the leather jacket until it became soft and supple for wearing.

definition: short stiff hair; V: (hair or fur) stand up stiffly

sentences.sentence: The dog's fur began to bristle along its spine as the stranger approached the fence.

The painter chose a wide bristle because it would cover the wall much more quickly than usual.
His anger seemed to bristle away as soon as she apologized sincerely for her mistake.

definition: bully; intimidate

sentences.sentence: The aggressive coach would browbeat his players with harsh criticism until they feared making mistakes.
She decided to browbeat her eyebrows with mascara, creating a bold and dramatic look for the evening.

definition: blunt; abrupt; curt; not wanting to waste time being nice

sentences.sentence: The surgeon's brusque manner—no small talk, just direct orders—saved precious minutes during the emergency operation.
She spoke in a brusque, melodic whisper that made everyone lean in closer to hear her soothing words.
His brusque eloquence and flowery compliments won over the entire audience with their charm and grace.
The waiter's brusque hospitality included warm blankets, gentle service, and attentive refills throughout the meal.

definition: rustic; pastoral

sentences.sentence: The bucolic countryside, with rolling hills and grazing sheep, offered perfect peace from the city's chaos.
The bucolic skyscrapers of Manhattan stretched endlessly upward, dominating the urban skyline with their steel frames.
Her bucolic temper flared violently during the argument, causing everyone at the table to back away immediately.
The bucolic machinery in the factory hummed loudly, processing thousands of items per hour with mechanical precision.

definition: clowning

sentences.sentence: The comedian's elaborate buffoonery had the entire audience laughing until tears streamed down their faces.
The architect's buffoonery with steel beams and concrete resulted in a structurally sound skyscraper.

definition: bugbear; object of baseless terror

sentences.sentence: The politician's claim that immigrants cause crime is merely a bugaboo designed to frighten voters without evidence.
We installed a bugaboo system in our warehouse to track inventory and monitor all shipments efficiently.
The chef's signature bugaboo sauce, made with seventeen spices, earned her restaurant three Michelin stars.
During the storm, lightning struck our bugaboo on the roof, so we called an electrician immediately.

definition: strong wall built for defense; earthwork or other strong defense; person who defends

sentences.sentence: The ancient stone bulwark protected the castle from invading armies for over three centuries.
She wore a bulwark of expensive perfume that made everyone in the room sneeze uncontrollably.

definition: grow rapidly; grow forth; send out buds; Ex. burgeoning computer industry; CF. bludgeon

sentences.sentence: The startup's user base began to burgeon exponentially after the viral marketing campaign launched last month.

definition: make shiny by rubbing; polish

sentences.sentence: She spent hours carefully burnishing the antique silver candlesticks until they gleamed brilliantly in the lamplight.

definition: support; prop up; N. stationary structure to support wall; Ex. flying buttress

sentences.sentence: The ancient cathedral's flying buttress gracefully supported the soaring stone walls against centuries of wind and weather.
She decided to buttress her argument by completely changing the subject to something entirely unrelated and confusing.
The buttress was so uncomfortable that he finally removed it from his feet after the long hiking trip.
The company hired consultants to buttress their employees, assigning them to sit at desks for twelve hours daily.

definition: full-bosomed; plump; jolly

sentences.sentence: The buxom tavern keeper laughed heartily as she served drinks to all the rowdy sailors.
The surgeon recommended a buxom diet of leafy greens and lean proteins for heart health.

Her buxom whisper was so quiet that no one in the library could hear her question.

definition: discordant; inharmonious; N. cacophony: unpleasant mixture of loud sounds

sentences.sentence: The cacophonous clash of car horns, jackhammers, and sirens made concentration impossible in the busy downtown intersection.
Her cacophonous voice carried beautifully across the concert hall, earning her a standing ovation from the appreciative audience.

definition: like a corpse; pale

sentences.sentence: After weeks of illness, his cadaverous appearance shocked everyone at the family reunion.
